items.title: Fibonacci: naïve recursion (overlap) vs DP (collapsed)
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  subgraph Naive["Naïve recursion: fib(5) — 15 nodes, work doubles per level"]
    F5["fib(5)"] --> F4["fib(4)"]
    F5 --> F3a["fib(3)"]
    F4 --> F3b["fib(3)"]
    F4 --> F2a["fib(2)"]
    F3a --> F2b["fib(2)"]
    F3b --> F2c["fib(2)"]
  end
  subgraph DP["With memo (top-down) or table (bottom-up): 6 unique calls"]
    DP0["fib(0)=0"] --> DP1["fib(1)=1"] --> DP2["fib(2)=1"] --> DP3["fib(3)=2"] --> DP4["fib(4)=3"] --> DP5["fib(5)=5"]
  end
items.caption: Overlapping sub-problems are computed many times in the naïve tree; DP stores each unique sub-result once → O(2ⁿ) collapses to O(n).

items.title: Bottom-up DP table for edit distance — `dp[i][j]`
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR Start["dp[0][0] = 0 (empty → empty)"] Start --> Row["Fill row 0: dp[0][j] = j (insertions)"] Row --> Col["Fill column 0: dp[i][0] = i (deletions)"] Col --> Cell["For each (i, j): dp[i][j] = min(
dp[i-1][j] + 1, // delete
dp[i][j-1] + 1, // insert
dp[i-1][j-1] + (a[i] ≠ b[j]) // replace
)"] Cell --> Ans["answer = dp[m][n]"]
items.caption: Iterate sub-problems from smallest to largest; each cell uses three previously computed neighbours.
